zh.lifeSummary: 當Elisabeth av Spoleto Von Ravensburg-Bechte於1121年出生在巴登阿赤 普福倫多福時，她的父親Berthold IV von Eberstein26歲，母親Uta von Calw-Sindelfingen genannt Herzogin von Schauenburg17歲。她和Rudolf von Bregenz-Pfullendorf至少有1個兒子和1個女兒。她去世於1164年5月8日在奧地利沃拉爾格布雷根茨享年43歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Elisabeth av Spoleto Von Ravensburg-Bechte于1121年出生在巴登阿赤 普福倫多福时，她的父亲Berthold IV von Eberstein26岁，母亲Uta von Calw-Sindelfingen genannt Herzogin von Schauenburg17岁。她和Rudolf von Bregenz-Pfullendorf至少有1个儿子和1个女儿。她去世于1164年5月8日在奥地利沃拉爾格布雷根茨享年43岁。

nameMeanings.name: Von
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Cambodian: written វ៉ុន, unexplained.

variants.name: Vaughan
meanings: Welsh: from fychan, a lenited form of bychan, a diminutive of bach ‘little’. This was often used to distinguish the younger of two bearers of the same personal name, typically the son of a father with the same name.

nameMeanings.name: Elisabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

variants.name: Elizabeth
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

nameMeanings.name: av
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of any of the names beginning with this syllable.

variants.name: Alexander
meanings: From the Latin form of the Greek name Alexandros, from alexein ‘to defend’ + anēr ‘man, warrior’ (genitive andros). The name became extremely popular in the post-classical period, and was borne by several individuals in the New Testament and some early Christian saints. Its use as a common given name throughout Europe, however, derives largely from the fame of Alexander the Great , King of Macedon ( 356–323 bc ), around whom a large body of popular legend grew up in late antiquity, much of which came to be embodied in the medieval ‘Alexander romances’.

variants.name: Alan
meanings: Of Celtic origin and uncertain derivation (possibly a diminutive of a word meaning ‘rock’). It was introduced into England by Breton followers of William the Conqueror , most notably Alan , Earl of Brittany, who was rewarded for his services with vast estates in the newly conquered kingdom. In Britain the variants Allan and Allen are considerably less frequent, and generally represent transferred uses of surname forms, whereas in America all three forms of the name are approximately equally common. See also Alun .

variants.name: Albert
meanings: From an Old French name, Albert, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from adal ‘noble’ + berht ‘bright, famous’. This was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to England, displacing the Old English form Æþelbeorht. The name is popular in a variety of forms in Western Europe, and has been traditional in a number of European princely families. It was out of favour in England for centuries, however, and the revival of its popularity in the 19th century was largely in honour of Queen Victoria's consort, Prince Albert of Saxe-Coburg-Gotha .

en.lifeSummary: When Elisabeth av Spoleto Von Ravensburg-Bechte was born in 1121, in Amt Pfullendorf, Baden, her father, Berthold IV von Eberstein, was 26 and her mother, Uta von Calw-Sindelfingen genannt Herzogin von Schauenburg, was 17. She had at least 1 son and 1 daughter with Rudolf von Bregenz-Pfullendorf. She died on 8 May 1164, in Bregenz, Vorarlberg, Austria, at the age of 43.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Elisabeth av Spoleto Von Ravensburg-Bechte est née en 1121, à Amt Pfullendorf, Baden, son père, Berthold IV von Eberstein, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Uta von Calw-Sindelfingen genannt Herzogin von Schauenburg, avait 17 ans. Elle a eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille avec Rudolf von Bregenz-Pfullendorf. Elle est décédée le 8 mai 1164, à Bregenz, Vorarlberg, Autriche, à l’âge de 43 ans.

de.Bad Herrenalb, Calw, Baden-Württemberg,: Bad Herrenalb, Kreis Calw, Baden-Württemberg, Deutschland
de.lifeSummary: Als Elisabeth av Spoleto Von Ravensburg-Bechte 1121 in Amt Pfullendorf, Baden geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Berthold IV von Eberstein 26 und ihre Mutter Uta von Calw-Sindelfingen genannt Herzogin von Schauenburg 17. Sie hatte mit Rudolf von Bregenz-Pfullendorf mindestens 1 Sohn und 1 Tochter. Sie starb am 8. Mai 1164 im Alter von 43 Jahren in Bregenz, Vorarlberg, Österreich.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Elisabeth av Spoleto Von Ravensburg-Bechte è nata il nel 1121 nel Amt Pfullendorf, Baden, sua padre, Berthold IV von Eberstein, aveva 26 anni e sua madre, Uta von Calw-Sindelfingen genannt Herzogin von Schauenburg, aveva 17 anni. Lei avuto almeno 1 figlio e 1 figlia con Rudolf von Bregenz-Pfullendorf. Morì il 8 maggio 1164, nel Bregenz, Vorarlberg, Austria, all'età di 43 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Elisabeth av Spoleto Von Ravensburg-Bechte(이)가 1121년 바든 아트 p프렌도르프에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Berthold IV von Eberstein(은)는 26세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Uta von Calw-Sindelfingen genannt Herzogin von Schauenburg(은)는 17세였다. 그녀는 Rudolf von Bregenz-Pfullendorf(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1164년 5월 8일 오스트리아 보라를버그 브레겐즈에서 43세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Elisabeth av Spoleto Von Ravensburg-Bechte родилась в 1121 г. в местности амт пфуллендорф, баден, её отцу по имени Berthold IV von Eberstein было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Uta von Calw-Sindelfingen genannt Herzogin von Schauenburg, 17. У неё был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь от Rudolf von Bregenz-Pfullendorf. Она умерла 8 мая 1164 г. в местности бредженц, форарлбирг, Австрия в возрасте 43 лет.
